picks.narrative: Playing on the road, SF's model quality signals outweigh the home-field headwind — the market over-adjusts for the travel disadvantage. Model prices SF at 37.0% to win; Pinnacle implies 30.9% (+224) — a 6.0-point gap. Both lineups are confirmed — all lineup-quality and handedness adjustments reflect the actual starting nine, not a projected estimate. Lineup confirmation typically improves model accuracy on those inputs. Key risk: Kyle Harrison (2.58 xFIP) is the sharper starter — a 0.47-point gap that has to be overcome by SF's other model signals. This pick requires those signals to hold.

A multi-factor convergence edge: Michael Soroka (3.29 xFIP) vs. Eric Lauer (5.41), with home field adding to the baseline. No single signal dominates — the probability gap of 6.3pp reflects several independent inputs each pushing in the same direction. The model's fair price is -118 vs. the +108 available. This is the second-highest-edge pick on today's slate (2 of 8). Note: ARI's lineup is projected, not confirmed — the lineup-quality adjustment uses a team-baseline blend. Historically, picks in this edge tier have gone 55–35 (+36.1% ROI) this season.
